taskDescription: Arrange the steps of describing a body structure in correct anatomical order, from establishing the reference point to specifying depth.
paragraphs: Establish the anatomical position: body upright, facing forward, palms facing forward, feet shoulder-width apart.
Identify the body plane for the view being described: sagittal (right/left), coronal (front/back), or transverse (upper/lower).
Use superior/inferior or anterior/posterior terms to describe the general region of the structure.
Apply medial/lateral terms to specify position relative to the midline.
Use proximal/distal terms when describing structures on the limbs relative to the trunk.
Specify whether the structure is superficial (closer to the surface) or deep (farther from the surface).

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
Good effort — review the areas you missed.
Excellent work! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
